summary: Family-level ownership inventory for root workspace_runtime_primitives.py behavior, used to keep command-generation-owned declarations out of hand-owned runtime semantics.
classification_rule: Prefer command-generation or declarative contracts for stable command/package metadata and generated projection behavior. Keep hand-owned only when behavior requires live workspace inspection, semantic judgment, provider integration, mutation safety, or package-specific runtime policy.

migrated_families.closure_role: supporting contract-owned migration and checker fail-closed proof

migration_policy.compatibility: Payload-compatible: the checker report keeps the same emitted AW primitive ownership shape while reading required primitive IDs from this contract.

families.title: Generated command/package proof metadata
families.runtime_role: Runtime code and check scripts may aggregate proof reports, but command/package truth, generated artifact maturity, target freshness, and proof metadata are declared in contracts or generated artifacts.
families.remaining_hand_owned_reason: Report assembly and changed-path selection still need runtime context; declarative command/package truth is not owned by workspace_runtime_primitives.py.

families.title: AW-owned primitive ID declarations consumed by generated-package proof
families.runtime_role: The generated-package checker derives required AW-owned primitive IDs from this inventory instead of a hand-authored Python constant.
families.remaining_hand_owned_reason: Primitive implementation and host support modules remain AW-owned package-domain behavior; only the required primitive-ID set moved to contract ownership.

families.title: Generated surface trust metadata and command routes
families.runtime_role: Runtime code may expose compact trust projections and changed-path reviews, but target identity, maturity, command route, and generated artifact provenance are generated/contract-owned.
families.remaining_hand_owned_reason: Changed-path interpretation and user-facing projection remain runtime aggregation; stable generated-surface facts are contract-backed.

families.title: Tiny and compact output projection schemas
families.owner_surface: src/agentic_workspace/contracts/schemas/* plus future generated projection helpers
families.runtime_role: workspace_runtime_primitives.py still contains many compact projection helpers that shape live payloads and selector responses.
families.remaining_hand_owned_reason: The current helpers combine schema shape, selector policy, and live runtime facts; migrate only after a generic projection primitive exists.

families.title: Shared projection and action-effect helpers
families.runtime_role: Small data-only helper module shared by startup, implement, proof, and report packet builders for authority-boundary and action-effect projection.
families.remaining_hand_owned_reason: The helpers preserve AW payload compacting and authority-boundary wording; they are split by owner boundary but not moved into command-generation semantics.

families.title: Start and startup runtime packet builders
families.runtime_role: Startup packet builders, selector hydration, skill routing, next-safe-action, and startup tiny/compact projections are behavior-owned by the startup runtime owner module.
families.remaining_hand_owned_reason: Startup/start-context entrypoints are extracted and no longer depend on private lookups into workspace_runtime_primitives.py; compatibility imports preserve existing private names.

families.title: Implement runtime packet builders and projections
families.runtime_role: Implement-context assembly, changed-path facts, acceptance/objective-drift projections, and implement tiny/compact projections are behavior-owned by the implement runtime owner module.
families.remaining_hand_owned_reason: Implement packet entrypoints are extracted and no longer depend on private lookups into workspace_runtime_primitives.py; compatibility imports preserve existing private names.

families.title: Planning continuation and active-state runtime packets
families.runtime_role: Planning continuation, active-state, safety-gate, candidate-pressure, and active execplan projections are owned by the Planning runtime packet module.
families.remaining_hand_owned_reason: Planning packet entrypoints are extracted and no longer depend on private lookups into workspace_runtime_primitives.py; compatibility imports preserve existing private names.

families.title: Proof, verification, and closeout runtime packet builders
families.runtime_role: Proof selection, verification aggregation, closeout obligations, and completion-contract projections are owned by the proof/closeout runtime module.
proof_refs: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_proof_cli.py tests/test_workspace_implement_cli.py tests/test_workspace_summary_cli.py -q
families.remaining_hand_owned_reason: Proof/verification/closeout packet entrypoints are extracted and no longer depend on private lookups into workspace_runtime_primitives.py; compatibility imports preserve existing private names.

families.title: Generated-surface trust runtime packets
families.runtime_role: Generated-surface trust projections, generated CLI freshness aggregation, selector requests, and tiny surface compatibility review are owned by the generated-surface runtime module.
families.remaining_hand_owned_reason: Generated-surface trust runtime extraction is complete for #1774; stable generated command/package truth remains contract-owned.

families.title: Intent, proof, and closeout semantic judgment
families.runtime_role: Host-specific and package-specific judgment over task intent, closeout trust, proof adequacy, and continuation ownership stays hand-owned.
families.remaining_hand_owned_reason: These functions interpret live user intent and repository state; hiding that judgment in generated code would move, not reduce, semantic ownership.

families.title: Provider integration and mutation safety behavior
families.runtime_role: Lifecycle mutation, payload provenance, subprocess/provider behavior, and destructive safety checks stay runtime-owned while issue #1656 covers provider/mutation boundary work.
families.remaining_hand_owned_reason: Mutation safety and provider truth require live side-effect policy and cannot be replaced by declarative command metadata alone.

families.title: Planning, Memory, and Verification package runtime boundaries
families.runtime_role: Package-specific Planning, Memory, and Verification behavior is already tracked in package-owned runtime inventories and related issues.
families.remaining_hand_owned_reason: Root AW should inventory these boundaries but not migrate package semantics under this issue.
